because.text: Third and last ROADMAP formal-verification target closed (after SizeBound 6b715124 and WaveAlgebra aeef1a79). MachineBytes.lean mirrors ser/deser of the oracle and proves serialize injectivity, round-trip + canonicity (a valid buffer is the UNIQUE serialization of its parse), §4.1 validation totality, 34|66 lengths, reserved-opcode-0x03 rejection, and the byte-0 glyph_eq discrimination - over a from-scratch FIPS 180-4 SHA-256 in core Lean (total: no partial/unsafe). Genesis identities H(I)/H(K)/H(S) (TV-1), the Canonical Invalid Object, and false_is_a_theorem (§5.2) are recomputed end-to-end serialize.sha256 and pinned to the spec constants by native_decide - the spec's own numbers now validate the in-Lean SHA-256. Bridge: no-sorry guard + FIPS vectors + 334-buffer executed-Lean-vs-oracle differential (every conformance CAS key incl. the malformed Era-1 0x03 object, genesis bytes, ~250 adversarial mutations) ALL AGREE. TCB honesty (README): SHA-256 correctness + pins ride native_decide + the FIPS/oracle differential; the structural theorems are symbolic. Proof grind delegated to a subagent iterating lean over SSH; maintainer cold-ran the full bridge + both prior-front regressions on a fresh master checkout on the mini before filing. Remaining unmechanized: a Lean reduction relation for redex recognition, and the step-tag classifier.

subject.note: Executable law: Book I byte-level machine correspondence machine-checked (Lean 4 + from-scratch FIPS 180-4 SHA-256)
